oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Distant Cousins - Peregrine Falcons and Parrots
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Darden, Jennifer
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 49 Number 4 (October/November 2022).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Peregrine Falcons’ speed and smarts attract a unique brand of bird watcher who forms a personal connection specifically to this species, which sometimes encourages them to participate in conservation efforts. Kids learn about peregrine falcons from nature shows and story time at schools when ambassadors like Equinox and Solstice visit. These live birds are valuable teaching tools that help Christine and her volunteers to gain attention and educate the public about these vulnerable birds of prey. It takes a movement of voices and an army of volunteers to create change. Christine exudes inspiration when she talks and teaches about her dual passions and shares stories of her hunting partners and rehab patients. She speaks with a belief that with each child who meets a peregrine falcon, every concerned citizen who rescues a raptor in crisis and each time a network of healers and helpers...Adult peregrine rehab patient. He flew into a stone seawall chasing a bird and fractured his wing. He was placed as an educational ambassador at another center as he was deemed non-releasable.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 49 No. 4 (2022): Watchbird October/November 2022; 18
